prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: Polyolefin encapsulants showed no chemical reaction with perovskite and have high electrical resistivity making them less prone to potential induced d
2. [SILVER] Source: Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat  | Statement: Butyl rubber edge seal has two orders of magnitude lower Water Vapor Transmission Rate compared to UV-curable epoxies and polymeric encapsulants.
3. [SILVER] Source: Damp Heat Test | Kiwa PVEL PV Module Reliability Scorecard | Statement: Only 69% of bills of materials tested degraded by less than 6% power loss after damp heat testing in the 2024 PVEL scorecard.
4. [SILVER] Source: Damp Heat Test | Kiwa PVEL PV Module Reliability Scorecard | Statement: Damp heat failures were attributed to delamination, junction box lids falling off, electrical insulation faults, and power loss.
5. [SILVER] Source: Damp Heat Test | Kiwa PVEL PV Module Reliability Scorecard | Statement: 15 manufacturers experienced at least one failure during damp heat testing in the 2024 reporting period.
